title: podman build  error creating new mount namespace
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT**

/kind bug

operation not permitted from create directory 

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.
root@32e35d5a8172:/azp# podman build   .
STEP 1: FROM ubuntu:20.10
STEP 2: USER 0
--> Using cache 8659dca2a86dd325d3033f5d58860e7731b8c0a8781fb9d98c8bcb6660f67c81
STEP 3: RUN mkdir app
error running container: error creating new mount namespace for [/bin/sh -c mkdir app]: operation not permitted
Error: error building at STEP "RUN mkdir app": error while running runtime: exit status 1

root@32e35d5a8172:/azp# cat Dockerfile
FROM ubuntu:20.10
USER 0
RUN mkdir app


**Describe the results you received:**

error running container: error creating new mount namespace for [/bin/sh -c mkdir app]: operation not permitted
Error: error building at STEP "RUN mkdir app": error while running runtime: exit status 1

**Describe the results you expected:**

build image

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

podman version 2.0.6

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Run docker in docker

comments.body: Can you provide additional details on "Run docker in docker" - is Podman being run inside a container? If so, please provide more details on the outer container.
You can only run podman within a privileged docker container at this time.
We have multiple podman in container issues open, lets keep discussion in one place. #9015

@rhatdan Has there been any progress on this? Our shared Gitlab CI runner does not allow us to set privileged mode and we're getting this exact error for a `build`. I don't think the linked issue is the same as this one.

```
error running container: error creating new mount namespace for [/bin/sh -c whoami]: operation not permitted
```

As a note: The command was run in a `debian-slim` image.

#10079 may have solved this; the owner of a user namespace should have the ability to create a new mount namespace.  If the `unshare` syscall is blocked, though, or the host system doesn't allow user namespaces to be created (i.e., if the `user.max_user_namespaces` sysctl setting is 0), we're kind of stuck.
Not sure we can do anything about user namespaces in a CI environment to be honest. Seems like we’re stuck.

@nalind it seems user namespaces configuration is not relevant. A clean Ubuntu Docker container won't run a `podman build` with `RUN` commands unless it gets privileged. Ubuntu usually has namespaces enabled by default.

In cases where the Docker context can't be modified (shared CI environments for example) it appears Podman cannot replace Docker yet.
